content.problemStatement:
Chef is responsible for maintaining a **dynamic list of values** (such as test scores, rankings, or performance metrics). As new values continue to stream in, Chef needs to **efficiently determine the current kkk-th largest value** at any given moment.
This allows Chef to constantly track thresholds—such as admission cut-offs, ranking limits, or qualification marks—in real time.

Chef wants to build a system that:


Initializes with an initial list of values.
Stores a position kkk, meaning Chef always wants to know the **kkk-th largest value so far**.
Allows adding new values dynamically.
After **each addition**, returns the updated **kkk-th largest value** among all values seen so far.
Your task is to help Chef implement such a tracker using an efficient data structure.

**Class Name**KthLargestKthLargestKthLargest

**Constructor Parameters**
kkk: The position kkk specifying which largest value Chef wants to track.
numsnumsnums: A reference to a vector containing the initial values.
**Member Function**
add()add()add():
Adds a new incoming value and returns the current **kkk-th largest value**.

Parameter : valvalval - value to be added


**Return Value**The class maintains an internal structure allowing Chef to dynamically update and query the kkk-th largest value at any time.

**Constraints**
1≤T≤100 1 \leq T \leq 100 1≤T≤100 — number of test cases
0≤N≤104 0 \leq N \leq 10^4 0≤N≤104 — number of initial values
1≤k≤N+Q 1 \leq k \leq N + Q 1≤k≤N+Q
−104≤nums[i]≤104 -10^4 \leq nums[i] \leq 10^4 −104≤nums[i]≤104
1≤Q≤104 1 \leq Q \leq 10^4 1≤Q≤104 — number of new values added per test case
−104≤newValue≤104 -10^4 \leq newValue \leq 10^4−104≤newValue≤104
At most 10410^4104 calls to add() per test case

Input Format


The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.




For each test case:




The first line contains an array of strings representing the list of operations:


`["KthLargest", "add", "add", ...]
`


The second line contains an array of arguments corresponding to each operation:


`[[k, initialArray], [value1], [value2], ...]
`





Output Format
For each test case:



For each of the QQQ added values, print a single integer:
the **current kkk-th largest value** after processing that addition.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Initial stream: [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8], k=4; 4th largest is 5.
Add 9: stream becomes [1..9], 4th largest is 6.
Add 10: stream [1..10], 4th largest is 7.
Add 11: stream [1..11], 4th largest is 8.
Add 12: stream [1..12], 4th largest remains 9.
